mapping.a_element: Siegel (genus-2) modular form whose Fourier coefficients are the counted objects
mapping.b_element: modular functions / Hauptmoduln attached to discrete subgroups of PSL2 (via Moonshine)

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is contour-integral extraction of BPS degeneracies from a Siegel modular form, with a moduli-dependent contour whose poles track the appearance/disappearance of two-centered bound states at walls of marginal stability; paper B's machinery is a group-theoretic McKay/Moonshine correspondence assigning discrete PSL2 subgroups to nodes of the affine E8 Dynkin diagram via elementary properties of PSL2. Beyond both living in the modular-forms/Moonshine area of mathematical physics, there is no role-for-role match: no shared contour/wall-crossing process, threshold, or governing equation drives both, so the overlap is setting-level vocabulary, not transferable machinery.
